document: Introduction To Internet Of Things JAN 2026

questions.questionText: The full form of MQTT is [cite: 189]
questions.explanation: MQTT is a lightweight, publish-subscribe network protocol specifically designed for restricted devices and low-bandwidth, high-latency, or unreliable networks. [cite: 201] It is the most common messaging protocol used in IoT because it allows sensors to communicate with servers using minimal power and data overhead. [cite: 202]

XMPP (Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol) primarily follows a client-server architecture. [cite: 216] Clients connect to a central server to send and receive messages. [cite: 217] Although it can support publish-subscribe features, its core design is client-server. [cite: 218]

questions.questionText: With respect to AMQP, which of the following message delivery guarantees allow each message to be delivered certainly but also may be delivered multiple times. [cite: 219]
questions.explanation: This delivery guarantee ensures that a message is successfully received by the consumer at least one time, eliminating the risk of data loss. [cite: 232, 235] However, if the sender does not receive a timely acknowledgment due to network issues, it will resend the message, which can result in the receiver processing the same data multiple times. [cite: 236] This approach prioritizes reliability over the avoidance of duplicates. [cite: 237]

questions.questionText: In MQTT for IoT, the Publishers are [cite: 238]
questions.explanation: In the MQTT protocol, Publishers are typically resource-constrained devices, such as lightweight sensors, that collect and send data to a central broker. [cite: 251] These sensors do not need to know which devices are receiving the information; they simply "publish" data to specific topics. [cite: 252] This design allows low-power devices to communicate efficiently over unstable networks without a direct connection to the end-user or subscriber. [cite: 253]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a method in MQTT? [cite: 254]
questions.explanation: In the MQTT protocol, Connect, Disconnect, and Publish are all fundamental control packets (methods) used to manage the communication cycle. [cite: 267] Connect establishes a link to the broker, Publish is used to send data to a specific topic, and Disconnect gracefully terminates the session. [cite: 268] Since all three are valid MQTT methods, none of the options provided are incorrect. [cite: 269]

COAP (Constrained Application Protocol) defines four messaging modes: Confirmable, Non-confirmable, Acknowledgement, and Reset. [cite: 292] These modes help ensure reliable and efficient communication in constrained devices. They control how messages are delivered and acknowledged. [cite: 293]

questions.questionText: State True or False. CoAP architecture is divided into three sub-layers. The messaging sub-layer is responsible for reliability and duplication of messages, while the request-response sub-layer is responsible for communication. The last layer, which is optional, is responsible for user registration. [cite: 294, 295, 296]
questions.explanation: The CoAP (Constrained Application Protocol) architecture is actually divided into two sub-layers, not three: the Messaging layer and the Request/Response layer. [cite: 311] The Messaging layer handles the reliability and retransmission of data, while the Request/Response layer manages the communication between clients and servers; [cite: 312] there is no official third "user registration" layer in the standard CoAP stack. [cite: 313]

questions.questionText: What is the full form of XMPP in loT? [cite: 314]
options: b. Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol [cite: 316]
questions.explanation: XMPP is a communication protocol based on XML that was originally designed for instant messaging and "presence" (knowing if a device is online). [cite: 326] In the context of IoT, its extensible nature makes it ideal for real-time data exchange and managing large-scale networks of devices. [cite: 327] It allows for secure, decentralized communication between sensors and servers across different platforms. [cite: 328]

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement if True or False. Statement: XMPP is not an open standard protocol. [cite: 329]
questions.explanation: XMPP is a widely recognized open standard protocol, originally developed by the Jabber community and later standardized by the IETF (Internet Engineering Task Force). [cite: 337] Because it is open-source, anyone can implement XMPP-based software without paying licensing fees, which has led to its extensive use in instant messaging and decentralized IoT networks. [cite: 338] This openness ensures compatibility and continuous development by a global community of contributors. [cite: 339]

questions.questionText: What is the full is/are not frame type of the AMQP protocol? [cite: 342]
questions.explanation: In the AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol) specification, the actual frame types are categorized as Method, Content Header, and Content Body. [cite: 347] Terms like "Origin," "Destination," and "Advance" are not recognized frame types in the AMQP technical architecture. [cite: 348] These frames are used to manage the delivery and structure of data as it moves between the client and the broker. [cite: 349]

questions.questionText: What is the basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol? [cite: 350]
questions.explanation: The basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol is called a Frame. [cite: 363] These frames are used to encapsulate all information sent between the client and the broker, including control commands and the actual message payload. [cite: 364] Because "Frame" is the technical term for the fundamental building block of AMQP communication, none of the other options provided are correct. [cite: 365]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are exchange types in AMQP? [cite: 368]
questions.explanation: In AMQP, an exchange is responsible for routing messages to various queues using specific rules. [cite: 379] Direct exchanges route messages based on an exact routing key match, Fan-out exchanges broadcast messages to all available queues, and Topic exchanges use wildcard matching for more flexible routing. [cite: 380] These distinct types allow developers to create complex and efficient messaging patterns within an IoT network. [cite: 381]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not an application of 6LoWPAN? [cite: 382]
questions.explanation: 6LoWPAN (IPv6 over Low-Power Wireless Personal Area Networks) is a fundamental technology designed specifically to enable the smallest, most power-constrained devices to connect to the internet. [cite: 394] It is widely used in both IoT (like smart homes) and IIoT (industrial monitoring and automation) because it allows standard IPv6 packets to travel over low-power wireless networks. [cite: 395]

The OSI (Open Systems Interconnection) model is a standardized framework that consists of 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, and Application. [cite: 402] This model is used to characterize and standardize the communication functions of a telecommunication or computing system without regard to its underlying internal structure and technology. [cite: 403]

questions.questionText: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents which of the following? [cite: 404]
options: a. The destination node address of the packet [cite: 405]
b. The intermediate hop in the network [cite: 406]
questions.explanation: In an IPv4 packet header, the Destination Address field contains the unique IP address of the final recipient node that is intended to receive the data. [cite: 409] While the packet may pass through many intermediate hops (routers) to reach its goal, those intermediate addresses are not stored in this specific field; [cite: 410] they are handled at the Data Link layer using MAC addresses. [cite: 411]
